phase: Phase 6 — Data flywheel (scrapers + crowdsource + archive synthesis)
files_changed: backend/app/lib/scrapers/base.py (BaseScraper + ScraperResult + register_scraper + run_all with INSERT-OR-IGNORE upsert)
backend/app/lib/scrapers/pagasa_synop.py (CSV-seeded PAGASA daily summaries; stdlib csv parsing)
backend/data/pagasa_seed.csv (12 PAGASA-style rows: 6 dauin_muck + 6 apo_reef, May-July)

bugs_found_and_fixed.issue: Orchestrator used per-row dedupe SELECT — O(N) queries and didn't dedupe within a single batch, causing IntegrityError on duplicate (site, date, source) within the same scraper run.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Switched to SQLite INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING with rowcount to compute inserted vs skipped in a single statement.

bugs_found_and_fixed.issue: PAGASA scraper used naive line.split(',') which broke on comments containing commas (e.g. 'calm day, dive conditions nominal').
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Switched to the stdlib csv module with DictReader.

bugs_found_and_fixed.issue: PAGASA scraper didn't filter by site_key — when CSV had rows for both dauin_muck and apo_reef, fetch('dauin_muck') returned all rows.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added up-front site_key filter so each fetch only returns rows for the requested site.

bugs_found_and_fixed.issue: Initial CSV didn't quote fields with embedded commas, so 4 of 6 dauin_muck rows were silently dropped by the parser.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Quoted all comments fields in the seed CSVs.

how_to_extend_phase6: Drop real PAGASA exports into data/pagasa_seed.csv (schema documented in scraper docstring).
Replace VizAppScraper / DiveVizScraper CSV fallback with browser automation (Playwright / Selenium) once client-side rendering is understood.
Add an NOAA NDBC buoy scraper (NDBC publishes realtime + historical text files at ndbc.noaa.gov) for raw wave + swell data.
Wire scrapers into a Windows Task Scheduler or GitHub Action cron for daily ingestion.
next_phase: Phase 7 — Calibration (Platt / isotonic on holdout to make '73% probability' actually mean 73% historical accuracy at that confidence level)
